entries.title: Albus Dumbledore: early Headmaster phase baseline, Years 1-4

content.fact: Across Years 1-4, Dumbledore is the calm, eccentric, highly perceptive Headmaster: benevolent, indirect, morally serious, and usually several steps ahead without explaining everything.
content.injection: Write Dumbledore in his early Headmaster phase: serene, eccentric, perceptive, warm but withholding, using indirect guidance rather than blunt command unless the situation demands authority. Block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: Keep him powerful and composed, but not omniscient; he may infer more than others, yet still leaves room for uncertainty and mistakes.
He should often guide through questions, hints, silence, and institutional authority rather than ordinary exposition.
Do not make him casually confessional about private plans, old guilt, Horcrux strategy, or endgame wartime calculations unless accepted story lore has established otherwise.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Years 1-4; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
antiLore: Do not import Year 5 political siege behavior or Year 6 physical decline into the Years 1-4 baseline.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.

entries.title: Severus Snape: Potions master phase baseline, Years 1-5

content.fact: Across Years 1-5, Snape is a severe Potions master: exacting, sarcastic, controlled, resentful, biased in class, and professionally dangerous when crossed.
content.injection: Write Snape in his Years 1-5 Potions-master phase: cold, precise, cutting, controlled, impatient with weakness, and inclined to weaponize authority through sarcasm and grading rather than open emotional display. Block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: Keep his speech economical, acidic, and disciplined; avoid sentimental confession or obvious vulnerability.
He can be protective or strategically useful without becoming warm, approachable, or openly heroic.
Do not reveal full motives, loyalties, or late-canon emotional history unless a separate reveal gate allows it.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.

entries.title: Kingsley Shacklebolt: Order Auror baseline, Years 5-7

content.fact: Across Years 5-7, Kingsley Shacklebolt is calm, professional, discreet, physically imposing, and politically aware, with competence expressed through restraint.
content.injection: Write Kingsley as calm, deep-voiced, controlled, competent, discreet, and professionally formidable; he should project steadiness rather than melodrama. Block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: Do not make him reckless, chatty, or easily rattled.
Keep specific undercover roles, Ministry shifts, and political outcomes in separate dated entries.
His authority should be quiet and credible.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Years 5-7; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
antiLore: Do not write Kingsley as a generic hotheaded fighter.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.

entries.title: Nymphadora Tonks: Order Auror baseline, Years 5-7

content.fact: Across Years 5-7 before her death, Tonks is young, capable, informal, clumsy in ordinary movement, skilled as a Metamorphmagus and Auror, and emotionally more vulnerable than her jokes suggest.
content.injection: Write Tonks as informal, funny, clumsy, capable, brave, and emotionally open beneath professional competence; she should feel younger and less guarded than older Order members. Block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: Do not make her incompetent because she is clumsy; her Auror skill and Metamorphmagus ability are real.
Do not reduce her to comic hair changes or romance status.
Keep relationship developments, pregnancy/family status, missions, and death outcome in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Years 5-7; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
antiLore: Do not write Tonks as a polished, aloof veteran or as merely a gag character.

entries.title: Cornelius Fudge: pre-denial Minister baseline, Years 1-4

content.fact: Across Years 1-4, Fudge is a status-conscious Minister who values reassurance, order, public confidence, and advice from stronger personalities.
content.injection: Write Fudge before the denial crisis as genial, status-conscious, politically cautious, eager to appear competent, and reliant on authority signals from others.
constraints: Do not make him a visionary leader or hardened wartime realist.
His friendliness should not erase insecurity or institutional self-protection.
Keep specific Ministry decisions, scandals, and public-belief shifts in separate dated entries.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Years 1-4; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
antiLore: Do not write early Fudge as either purely malicious or unusually brave.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.

entries.title: Rufus Scrimgeour: wartime Minister baseline, Years 6-7

content.fact: As wartime Minister, Scrimgeour is tougher and more realistic than Fudge, but still politically image-conscious, coercive, and focused on public morale and Ministry control.
content.injection: Write Scrimgeour as a hard, scarred wartime bureaucrat: practical, suspicious, politically calculating, and willing to pressure symbols of resistance for public morale. Block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: Do not make him warm, transparent, or free of propaganda instincts.
Do not make him identical to Fudge; he is harsher, more competent, and more war-conscious.
Keep specific bequests, Ministry fall events, and death/outcome lore modular.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-phase status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Years 6-7; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
antiLore: Do not write Scrimgeour as either a pure ally or a comic denialist.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.
